chunks.text: In December 1955, on a bus in Montgomery, Alabama
a black woman named Rosa Parks was ordered to give her seat to a white passenger.
How short, how quiet those words were!
Yet her single word moved the history of America.
She was arrested on the spot.
In those days, the law separated the races
and black people could only sit in certain seats.
The law, which had made discrimination a system, had long cast a shadow on society.
Parks' refusal sparked the Montgomery Bus Boycott.
Reverend Martin Luther King led the way
and the black citizens of the town stopped riding the buses.
What a strong protest it was!
the Supreme Court would declare that racial separation on the buses was unconstitutional.
The " No " of a single citizen changed the whole of society.
Let us never forget such a single word, filled with courage.
